arts.ttl: Transfer news and rumours today: Tristan Crama to Millwall, Hannes Delcroix to Oxford United, Matej Jurasek to Norwich City
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up the latest transfer news and rumours, including Tristan Crama to Millwall, Hannes Delcroix to Oxford and Matej Jurasek to Norwich City.

arts.ttl: Hull City owner Acun Ilıcalı holds transfer talks with Brazilian – Looking to seal deal
arts.sum: There’s a claim in the Turkish media that Hull City are looking to finalise a deal for Fenerbahce attacker Lincoln. The Brazilian has been linked with a move to the Championship side in the past week as Hull City owner Acun Ilıcalı looks to use his influence in his role as vice-president of Fenerbahce. Yağız The post Hull City owner Acun Ilıcalı holds transfer talks with Brazilian – Looking to seal deal appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Millwall land former Brighton and Hull forward after ripping up Sunderland contract
arts.sum: Millwall have signed forward Aaron Connolly on a permanent deal from Championship side Sunderland. Alex Neil had room for a forward in his squad with Tom Bradshaw, 32, sealing a switch to Championship rivals Oxford after passing a medical on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Hull City handed quadruple injury boost ahead of crucial Millwall trip
arts.sum: Incoming arrival Nordin Amrabat is expected to be involved, while he faces a big decision in goal over whether to play Ivor Pandur or hand Carl Rushworth a league debut after he made his long-awaited Tigers bow in the FA Cup. Though the Tigers will be without long-term absentees Kasey Palmer, Charlie Hughes, Dogukan Sinik, Liam Millar and Mohamed Belloumi, plus the suspended Oscar Zambrano, having those players available is a welcome shot in the arm for Ruben Selles ahead of a busy three-game week which also sees City host QPR on Tuesday night before facing Sheffield United at Bramall Lane next Friday night.

arts.ttl: Hull City agree surprise deal for versatile 37-year-old
arts.sum: Hull City are set to sign free agent Nordin Amrabat after reaching an agreement over a short-term deal,… The post Hull City agree surprise deal for versatile 37-year-old appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Hull City set to seal third January transfer deal as veteran attacker arrives
arts.sum: The brother of Sofyan, the former Manchester United midfielder who now plays in Turkey with Fenerbahce, Amrabat is a right winger with a CV that includes spells with the likes of PSV, Galatasaray, Malaga and Watford amid an extensive list of former clubs that has hit double figures throughout a career spanning the best part of two decades. The veteran winger, who turns 38 at the end of March, most recently played for AEK Athens in the Greek Super League, where he scored once in six appearances this season before agreeing to terminate his deal to facilitate a return to England.

arts.ttl: Preview: Millwall vs. Hull City - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Championship clash between Millwall and Hull City, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: 'Its annoying' - Hull City ace admits big frustration about Tigers form - Hull Live
arts.sum: "I've not really thought about it like that, to be fair, but yes, it makes sense what you said there (about not creating momentum)," Slater told Hull Live. I think he's right in saying that he just needs that little bit more from us, and then it probably will happen."

arts.ttl: Winger in ‘advanced negotiations’ to join Hull City – Confidence around surprise move
arts.sum: Nordin Amrabat has been linked with a move to Hull City at multiple points throughout his very varied career. Now at the ripe old age of 37, he’s ready for his second experience in English football, having played for Watford from 2016 to 2018.
